From an extremely left-wing economic perspective, the stability of the entire economic system (and therefore the well-being of the collective) is best served when the state keeps inflation firmly under control, even if that means tolerating higher unemployment. Inflation erodes the real value of wages and savings, hits the poorest hardest, and can undermine the state’s ability to plan and redistribute resources. Unemployment, while painful, can be addressed through robust public-sector job programs, universal basic income, or other redistributive mechanisms once prices are stable. Hence, prioritizing inflation control aligns with the left-wing imperative to preserve the integrity of state-managed economic structures.

Therefore the statement is rejected outright.
